COMPARISON:  ___ ultrasound  FINDINGS:   LIVER: The liver is coarsened and nodular in echotexture. The contour of the liver is nodular, consistent with cirrhosis.  There is a 1.6 cm echogenic focus within the mid right hepatic lobe, peripherally, for

EXAMINATION:  CHEST (PA AND LAT)  INDICATION:  ___ with h/o cirrhosis c/o 3 days worsening ruq abdominal pain// ?pneumonia  COMPARISON:  ___  FINDINGS:   PA and lateral views of the chest provided.  There is blunting of the right CP angle indicative of a small effusion.  Otherwise the lungs are clear.  Cardiomediastinal silhouette appears normal.  Bony structures are intact.  No free air below the right hemidiaphragm.  IMPRESSION:   Tiny right pleural effusion, otherwise unremarkable exam.

EXAMINATION:  CT abdomen and pelvis  INDICATION:  ___ with h/o cirrhosis and multiple abdominal surgeries c/o 3 days worsening ruq abdominal pain and vomiting  TECHNIQUE:  Single phase split bolus contrast: MDCT axial images were acquired through the abdomen and pelvis following intravenous contrast administration with split bolus technique. Oral contrast was administered.  Coronal and sagittal reformations were performed and reviewed on PACS.  DOSE:  Total DLP (Body) = 665 mGy-cm.  COMPARISON:

抽取_Finding.name: 抽取_Finding
examples: liver coarsened and nodular echotexture; nodular liver contour; 1.6 cm echogenic focus in mid right hepatic lobe; normal hepatopetal flow in main portal vein; large volume ascites; no intrahepatic biliary dilation; no gallstones; limited visualization of pancreas due to bowel gas; splenomegaly; no hydronephrosis in right kidney; aorta and IVC within normal limits
blunting of the right costophrenic angle; lungs clear; normal cardiomediastinal silhouette; intact bony structures; no free air below right hemidiaphragm

trace right pleural effusion; atelectasis in right lower lobe; multiple periesophageal varices near GE junction; nodular and heterogeneous liver enhancement; extensive portosystemic varices; partially distended gallbladder containing a stone; no biliary ductal dilation; normal pancreas; normal spleen size without focal abnormality; splenule; patent splenic vein; normal adrenal glands; renal cortical hypodensities suggestive of simple cysts; moderate volume ascites partially loculated in right upper quadrant; mildly distended small bowel loops without obstruction; normal appendix; minimally thickened colon in right upper quadrant; partially distended urinary bladder; normal prostate size; no lymphadenopathy; aortoiliac calcification; degenerative disc disease at L5-S1
partially occlusive thrombus within the main portal vein; large volume ascites; worsening splenomegaly; 1.6 cm focus within the right hepatic lobe

liver coarsened and nodular echotexture; no focal liver mass; main portal vein patent with hepatopetal flow; large ascites; right and left portal veins patent with antegrade flow; main hepatic artery patent with appropriate waveform; right, middle and left hepatic veins patent with appropriate waveforms; splenic vein patent with antegrade flow; no intrahepatic biliary dilation; contracted gallbladder with cholelithiasis; normal pancreas; normal spleen echogenicity; normal cortical echogenicity and corticomedullary differentiation in kidneys; no masses, stones, or hydronephrosis in kidneys; aorta and IVC within normal limits

temporal_info.name: temporal_info
examples: procedure duration:total intra-service time of 13 min

quantity_info.name: quantity_info
examples: Lactulose 30 mL DAILY; Nadolol 40 mg DAILY; Pravastatin 40 mg QPM

Lactulose 30 mL TID; Furosemide 20 mg DAILY; Spironolactone 50 mg DAILY; Famotidine 20 mg Q12H; Oxycodone 10 mg Q6H; Multivitamins 1 TAB DAILY; Potassium Chloride 10 mEq DAILY; Sulfameth/Trimethoprim SS 1 TAB DAILY; Calcium carbonate-vitamin D3 600 mg DAILY; Polyethylene Glycol 17 g DAILY; Heparin drip; Morphine 4 mg IV x2; Ondansetron 4 mg IV

impression.name: impression
examples: Post TIPS monitoring for congenital hepatic fibrosis with portal hypertension and variceal bleeding. No hepatic encephalopathy noted. Mild post-op complication resolved with albumin. Liver function tests elevated but improving.

Cirrhosis with new partially occlusive thrombus within the main portal vein, large volume ascites, worsening splenomegaly, and a new 1.6 cm echogenic focus in the right hepatic lobe requiring follow-up MR.
Tiny right pleural effusion, otherwise unremarkable exam.

Cirrhosis with portal hypertension, moderate ascites partially loculated in right upper quadrant, extensive portosystemic varices, new partially occlusive thrombus in main portal vein, gallbladder stone, small bowel distention likely ileus, mild proximal colon thickening suggesting portal colopathy, trace right pleural effusion with chronic atelectasis in right lower lung, and extensive atherosclerotic disease of the aorta.

Patient with Child Class C cirrhosis due to alcohol and untreated hepatitis C (genotype 3), complicated by esophageal varices, ascites, and hepatic encephalopathy, presenting with acute on chronic right upper quadrant abdominal pain, vomiting, and diarrhea likely due to viral gastroenteritis. Imaging shows new partially occlusive portal vein thrombosis, large volume ascites, worsening splenomegaly, and a new 1.6 cm hepatic lesion requiring follow-up MRI. Hospital course complicated by hyponatremia and non-occlusive portal vein thrombosis; anticoagulation was deferred due to compliance concerns. Patient treated with IV fluids, pain control, antiemetics, and heparin drip. Discharged home with stable condition and instructions for follow-up.

indication.name: indication
examples: Post TIPS monitoring for congenital hepatic fibrosis with portal hypertension and variceal bleeding.
Right upper quadrant abdominal pain with history of cirrhosis, suspected portal venous thrombosis.
Right upper quadrant abdominal pain with history of cirrhosis, suspected pneumonia.
Right upper quadrant abdominal pain and vomiting with history of cirrhosis and multiple abdominal surgeries.
Right upper quadrant abdominal pain, vomiting, and diarrhea in a patient with cirrhosis and history of multiple abdominal surgeries.

trace right pleural effusion; atelectasis in right lower lobe; multiple periesophageal varices near GE junction; nodular and heterogeneous liver enhancement; extensive portosystemic varices; partially distended gallbladder containing a stone; no biliary ductal dilation; normal pancreas; normal spleen size without focal abnormality; splenule; patent splenic vein; normal adrenal glands; renal cortical hypodensities suggestive of simple cysts; moderate volume ascites partially loculated in right upper quadrant; mildly distended small bowel loops without obstruction; normal appendix; minimally thickened colon in right upper quadrant; partially distended urinary bladder; normal prostate size; no lymphadenopathy; aortoiliac calcification; degenerative disc disease at L5-S1
partially occlusive thrombus within the main portal vein; large volume ascites; worsening splenomegaly; 1.6 cm focus within the right hepatic lobe

impression.name: impression
examples: Post TIPS monitoring for congenital hepatic fibrosis with portal hypertension and variceal bleeding. No hepatic encephalopathy noted. Mild post-op complication resolved with albumin. Liver function tests elevated but improving.

Cirrhosis with new partially occlusive thrombus within the main portal vein, large volume ascites, worsening splenomegaly, and a new 1.6 cm echogenic focus in the right hepatic lobe requiring follow-up MR.
Tiny right pleural effusion, otherwise unremarkable exam.

Cirrhosis with portal hypertension, moderate ascites partially loculated in right upper quadrant, extensive portosystemic varices, new partially occlusive thrombus in main portal vein, gallbladder stone, small bowel distention likely ileus, mild proximal colon thickening suggesting portal colopathy, trace right pleural effusion with chronic atelectasis in right lower lung, and extensive atherosclerotic disease of the aorta.

Patient with Child Class C cirrhosis due to alcohol and untreated hepatitis C (genotype 3), complicated by esophageal varices, ascites, and hepatic encephalopathy, presenting with acute on chronic right upper quadrant abdominal pain, vomiting, and diarrhea likely due to viral gastroenteritis. Imaging shows new partially occlusive portal vein thrombosis, large volume ascites, worsening splenomegaly, and a new 1.6 cm hepatic lesion requiring follow-up MRI. Hospital course complicated by hyponatremia and non-occlusive portal vein thrombosis; anticoagulation was deferred due to compliance concerns. Patient treated with IV fluids, pain control, antiemetics, and heparin drip. Discharged home with stable condition and instructions for follow-up.
